Menu / ui.vector-animation

Can Swift packages do vector animation playback?

also known as: lottie · after effects animation · bodymovin

Audio17 Images7 Media4 MIDI2 Networking9 Speech2 Storage14 Ui14 Video4 Voice1
PackageiOSmacOSwatchOStvOSvisionOSLinuxCatalyst
lottie-ios as of 4.6.1
Details
✓

serves it · confidence 0.90 · as of 4.6.1

  • LottieAnimationView
    Sources/Public/Animation/LottieAnimationView.swift:91
    View the line on GitHub →
    Core rendering view; unconditional decl, no platform gaps in surface digest.
  • LottieAnimationView.play
    Sources/Public/Animation/LottieAnimationView.swift:229
    View the line on GitHub →
    Playback entry point; unconditional, no platform gaps.
  • LottieAnimation.named
    Sources/Public/Animation/LottieAnimationHelpers.swift:39
    View the line on GitHub →
    Animation loading API; unconditional, no platform gaps.

not verified yet · confidence 0.25 · as of 4.6.1

  • Package.swift:7
    View the line on GitHub →
    Package.swift declares no watchOS platform floor (iOS/macOS/tvOS/visionOS only). Missing floor is not absence evidence, so status is unknown.
  • README.md:0
    View the line on GitHub →
    README platform list (iOS, macOS, tvOS, visionOS, Android, Web) omits watchOS.

not verified yet · confidence 0.20 · as of 4.6.1

  • README.md:0
    View the line on GitHub →
    README platform list omits Linux; 191 of 759 surface decls carry guards resolving absent on linux, and core files import QuartzCore unconditionally, but there is no guard/availability evidence of absence for the core symbols, so status is unknown.
